_comment: WC2026 Group J, Matchday 2 (AT&T Stadium, Arlington). MD1: Argentina 3-0 Algeria (Messi hat-trick); Austria 3-1 Jordan. Both on 3 points. FanDuel odds Argentina -185 (1.541) / Draw +310 (4.10) / Austria +550 (6.50); O/U 2.5. Calibrated in line with the market. Austria is a competitive European side (NOT a low block) -> open game, rho -0.05.

meta.stakes: Both won MD1 and sit on 3 points; the winner tops Group J and is all but qualified. Messi (16 WC goals) is one away from the outright all-time record.

home.name: Argentina
notes: World champion, elite attack (Messi hat-trick vs Algeria). attack 1.35.
XI CONFIRMED: full-strength side (Messi + Alvarez start) -> lambdas hold.
Calibrated in line with the market (~62%): don't underrate an elite favorite.
Top-tier defense (Romero, Lisandro Martinez, E. Martinez) -> low Austria lambda, defense 0.76.

away.name: Austria
notes: XI CONFIRMED: attacking 4-2-3-1 with Sabitzer + Kalajdzic (a real No. 9) -> competes, confirms the open game.
Solid, physical European side (Alaba, Laimer, Danso); beat Jordan 3-1. NOT a low block: it competes and plays -> open game (rho -0.05). attack 0.95.
Organized but not elite at the back (conceded to Jordan) -> defense 1.15.
Will go toe-to-toe rather than park the bus, so more goals than a parked-bus matchup.

cards._referee: Amin Mohamed Omar (Egypt), CONFIRMED. FIFA ref since 2017; career ~3.5-4.0 YC/match (moderate). International-context discount; small bump for a physical Austria. Not a lopsided block, so cards moderate.
